codeReview.summary: The commit adds the requested Phase 0 OCaml/Dune skeleton, wires the rgoc executable to print the required version string, includes a matching unit test, and the handoff evidence shows all Phase 0 validation-contract commands passed in the flake environment. I did not find correctness or contract gaps in the implementation diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The compiler-worker skill requires a red-to-green test-first cycle, but the transcript shows a greenfield Phase 0 skeleton was implemented and validated without a meaningful failing-test phase. The skill should explicitly allow skeleton/bootstrap features to create the initial test and implementation together when no runnable baseline exists.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for worker session d276302a-9484-4336-82a5-66512fff573a shows files were created before any failing test run, while handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges asks for this exact exception; .factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md step 3 currently states to make the new tests fail before implementing the feature.

summary: Reviewed commit 79c43bec178214c0b0faa99135fe336ce528618f for phase-0-project-skeleton-and-cli-version. The feature satisfies the Phase 0 milestone contract and no blocking or non-blocking code issues were identified.
